scenario_title: Add RR at a name that already owns a CNAME
test_rationale: This test creates a live CNAME owner and then issues a single UPDATE that merely verifies the name exists before adding an A RR at that same owner. The protocol gap is whether the server rejects the update, silently ignores it, or accepts it in a way that violates or changes the CNAME-only state. A follow-up ANY query on the affected owner makes any reply/state difference observable and keeps the scenario Ferret-executable.

group_1.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE that would place an A RR alongside an existing CNAME, typically preserving the original CNAME-only state.
group_1.post_state: QUERY ANY alias.campus.edu. returns only the CNAME rrset.

group_2.behavior: Processes the UPDATE differently under the underspecified conflict, potentially accepting the A addition or returning a different failure code.
group_2.post_state: QUERY ANY alias.campus.edu. may show both CNAME and A, or a changed owner-state compared with BIND.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2181 §10.1 requires that a CNAME owner name have no other data, but RFC 2136 §3.4.2 does not precisely specify the result when an UPDATE syntactically adds a non-CNAME RR at such an owner under a prerequisite that only proves the name exists. That leaves both the UPDATE RCODE and the post-update owner state underspecified.
